Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. I\xe2\x80\x99m still in New York, where dinner reservations are scarce but coffee shops open before 7 a.m. are plentiful. Today, Sarah Shapiro is here to walk you through this tariff business, and she also offers an assessment of the current situation at Nike, where Elliott Hill has been running the show for a few months now.

Feb 05, 2025

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. I\xe2\x80\x99m still in New York, where dinner reservations are scarce but coffee shops open before 7 a.m. are plentiful. Today, Sarah Shapiro is here to walk you through this tariff business, and she also offers an assessment of the current situation at Nike, where Elliott Hill has been running the show for a few months now. Be sure to email her at SShapiro@puck.news with all your feedback. Up top, I have a quick dispatch from last night\xe2\x80\x99s Marc Jacobs show at the New York Public Library.

\xf0\x9f\x9b\x8d\xef\xb8\x8f For those of you with the Shoppies : A lot of people have been asking me about the exit of well-regarded C.E.O. Marco Gobbetti from Salvatore Ferragamo on Monday. I\xe2\x80\x99ll get into this some day soon, but a few thoughts did cross my mind while wearing a Ferragamo dress ( this , which also comes in red ) and shoes ( these ) designed by its 28-year-old creative director, Maximilian Davis , to last weekend\xe2\x80\x99s 15 Percent Pledge Gala in Los Angeles. Ferragamo is an extremely challenging gig\xe2\x80\x94the business is family run, independent, and operates largely out of Florence, which makes putting together a strong team incredibly hard. (The departure of Gobbetti, a seasoned luxury exec whose last two gigs\xe2\x80\x94Burberry and this\xe2\x80\x94are two of the toughest jobs in the business, underscores this.) And yet, Davis is making thought-provoking, if imperfect, moves. Each season, the collections get better. If I lived in New York, I would buy this blouse .


Mentioned in this issue : Elliott Hill , Nike, Phil Knight , Bill Bowerman , Mark Parker , John Donahoe , Marc Jacobs, Beka Gvishiani , the de minimis exception, Hillary Kerr , Halie LeSavage , DSW, Zappos, Dillard\xe2\x80\x99s, Simeon Siegel , Foot Locker, Adidas, Under Armour, Donald Trump , Shein, Temu, Ssense, LVMH, Sidney Toledano , and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

PERFORMANCE UNLEASHED
With a distinct sporting personality, the Range Rover Sport is a peerless performer.
EXPLORE

Marc\xe2\x80\x99s the Spot

The entire New York fashion community and beyond, everyone from Style Not Com\xe2\x80\x99s Beka Gvishiani to LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s very own Sidney Toledano , showed up for Marc Jacobs\xe2\x80\x99 Fall/Winter 2025 collection at the New York Public Library on Monday night\xe2\x80\x94 days before the official start of the shows. And yet, right on time by every other measure: The models started walking just a few minutes after 7:30 p.m. and were done by 7:39.

Photo: Alexandra Arnold

But efficiency is table stakes for Jacobs these days. Harper\xe2\x80\x99 s Bazaar\xe2\x80\x99s Lynette Nylander and I shared our near-instant reactions to the clothes on the latest episode of Fashion People , and by now you\xe2\x80\x99ve combed through enough Instagram Stories to see that Jacobs continued interrogating the exaggerated paper doll silhouette, this time inflating each garment into something bulbous and unafraid to take up space. Lynette mentioned how much Jacobs loves fashion\xe2\x80\x94loves clothes \xe2\x80\x94and I\xe2\x80\x99ve always admired his ability to nod to other designers while being totally original. Yesterday, though, I liked the moments when he referenced himself . His flat-to-the-body sweater sets, corduroy trousers, and lace slip dresses\xe2\x80\x94reminiscent of various collections from the past three decades\xe2\x80\x94were zhuzhed into something more voluptuous; his signature pointy-toe flats elongated to near-oblivion. What\xe2\x80\x99s great about Marc Jacobs is that the runway not only still matters to the entire operation, but it\xe2\x80\x99s really the secret ingredient.

Jacobs also writes the best show notes in the business\xe2\x80\x94I hope he\xe2\x80\x99s working on a memoir\xe2\x80\x94and Monday\xe2\x80\x99s were no exception. \xe2\x80\x9cCourage\xe2\x80\xa6 I have come to understand that fear is not my enemy\xe2\x80\x94it is a necessary companion to creativity.\xe2\x80\x9d I keep very little from shows\xe2\x80\x94just a few invites and catalogs culled from nearly 20 years of attending these things\xe2\x80\x94but one of my most prized possessions is Jacobs\xe2\x80\x99 final note from his last bow at Louis Vuitton. Last night\xe2\x80\x99s missive, which also included a masthead featuring every member of his design team, earned a place in my slim file of fashion ephemera.

Now, here\xe2\x80\x99s Sarah\xe2\x80\xa6

Sarah Shapiro

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6 About Tariffs

  • The de minimis exception : The ubiquitous buzzwords sustainability and capsule collection have a new companion in the modern argot of the industry: the de minimis exception, which refers to imports or online orders valued at $800 or less that are able to enter the United States without the recipient paying tariffs or going through formal customs procedures and protocols. Shein and Temu have built their entire businesses around shipping inexpensive products from China that are covered by the de minimis exception. It\xe2\x80\x99s obviously another story for luxury retailers, like Ssense. When you order that Dries Van Noten slip dress for $975, you\xe2\x80\x99re going over the $800 threshold, so additional import taxes apply.

    Now, amid Trump \xe2\x80\x99s new tariff era, the de minimis exception is toast. For Mexico and Canada, tariffs have been paused for 30 days, but 10 percent levies are scheduled to go into effect for China today, and they include a provision that closes the de minimis loophole. All these goods will incur a tax on top of the purchase, likely causing retailers to meaningfully adjust their sales forecasts, prices, and more.
  • Resizing the cost stack : Meanwhile, retailers, analysts, and consumers are trying to decipher how the tariffs will impact prices and be passed on to buyers. Indeed, tariffs are just another cost input, albeit one wrapped in political drama, and retailers can manage them in three main ways. They can always just raise prices, which is the inflationary option. Or they could technically accept the reduced margins and try to offset them with increased volume\xe2\x80\x94a practical challenge for any fiscally responsible company operating at scale. The third option would require cutting costs elsewhere in the operating stack (supply chain, manufacturing, packaging, etcetera).

    The likely result of these tariffs is that strong brands, which offer beloved or essential products, will more easily be able to pass some of the new cost on to the customer. Others will be maneuvering through a game of margins, carefully reducing costs in nonessential areas\xe2\x80\x94say, paring back a splashy new marketing campaign or postponing a store renovation. But everyone is hoping that these new taxes are temporary, perhaps a negotiating lever with trade partners, and not the new status quo. (It\xe2\x80\x99s also not entirely clear that this is, strictly speaking, legal .)
  • Survivor: Retail Edition\xe2\x80\xa6 : Executives at the highest level of the fashion industry are always factoring exogenous risks, like recessions and natural disasters, into their business models and long-term planning. And these tariffs, while shocking, shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t have caught any C-suite totally off-guard, given that Trump talked about tariffs constantly during the campaign. There are a number of best-practice protocols for black swan economic environments, like this one, and I expect you\xe2\x80\x99ll see some of these initiatives rolled out across the retail industry, depending on how long this trade war lasts.

    First, retailers will consider diversifying their supplier base. Over the years, we\xe2\x80\x99ve seen companies de-lever their relationship with, say, China or Mexico by finding partners in South East Asia and South America. I expect this to continue, although it\xe2\x80\x99s a time-consuming and laborious process. One strategist I spoke with recalled the era when \xe2\x80\x9ctube tops\xe2\x80\x9d were taxed differently than \xe2\x80\x9cshirts,\xe2\x80\x9d an economic wrinkle that led to a profusion of the garment entering the country. In the A.I. era, retailers can more easily leverage data analytics to identify sweet spots in pricing where even a significant increase won\xe2\x80\x99t significantly impact sales volume, ultimately boosting profits despite potential tariff pressures.

    Also, I expect retailers will become more communicative about tax implications with their customers, perhaps by adding more FAQs or details to the shipping and returns section. Rather than allow customers to wonder if they\xe2\x80\x99re going to be impacted by customs or taxes when ordering from overseas, brands and companies should be forthcoming about the downstream economics. In the end, it will remove a hurdle before purchase. Who knows, maybe the de minimis exception will end up on a campaign poster one day. (Let\xe2\x80\x99s hope not\xe2\x80\xa6)

And now for the main event\xe2\x80\xa6

Can Nike Just Do It Again?

The $51 billion behemoth is staging a comeback with a new-old C.E.O. and a playbook they\xe2\x80\x99ve been running since the \xe2\x80\x9980s: innovate through imitation and use the competition as a catalyst for growth. Can they regain the market value of the past? Hoka, you\xe2\x80\x99ve been warned.

Sarah Shapiro

It\xe2\x80\x99s hard to distill the historic multidecade rise of Nike down to just a few sentences, but humor me. Back in the early \xe2\x80\x9970s, co-founder and former University of Oregon middle distance runner Phil Knight collaborated with his former coach Bill Bowerman on the so-called \xe2\x80\x9cMoon Shoe,\xe2\x80\x9d which quickly became the ne plus ultra product for serious joggers. Nike carefully expanded on its success in the performance running space by dominating the burgeoning sneaker industry and then subsequently moved into new categories, applying the same strategy each time: aligning itself with the professionals at the apex of each sport before moving downstream to capture all the value among the amateurs and hobbyists and other aspiring classes.

In the process, Knight helped architect an oft-imitated brand playbook for seizing markets from the top down, a distinctly novel idea in the era of mass market corporate icons like McDonald\xe2\x80\x99s and GE. In the ensuing decades, it\xe2\x80\x99s been deployed by companies as varied as Starbucks and Uber, Amazon and Tesla.

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

PERFORMANCE UNLEASHED
With a distinct sporting personality, the Range Rover Sport is a peerless performer.
EXPLORE

Nike expanded its purview from running to basketball to training, and then to pretty much every conceivable athletic activity and market sector\xe2\x80\x94golf, tennis, baseball, football, fashion, swimming, even leisure. At the dawn of this decade, its market cap peaked at more than a quarter-trillion dollars , making it significantly larger than every other business in the space combined. And that, of course, is where this story takes a slightly interesting turn.

In 2020, Knight\xe2\x80\x99s disciple Mark Parker stepped down as C.E.O. He was replaced by John Donahoe , an unflinchingly qualified executive and company board member, who had previously run Bain and eBay. One of Donahoe\xe2\x80\x99s first decisions\xe2\x80\x94perhaps fueled by the pandemic-accelerated flight to e-commerce\xe2\x80\x94was to end the company\xe2\x80\x99s distribution relationship with several key partners (Zappos, DSW, Dillard\xe2\x80\x99s, and Amazon) and go all-in on D.T.C.

On paper, it was a logical idea: Donahoe\xe2\x80\x99s plan would allow Nike to amass more data, control its channels, and dominate the end-to-end experience. And yet, as Lauren Sherman noted a few months ago in her excellent piece Sneakerheadaches , it was ultimately ill-fated. Unlike the luxury market, where multibrand retail continues to contract, venues like Dick\xe2\x80\x99s offer an essential service for a company like Nike.


Anyway, long story short, Donahoe had the wrong strategy, and implemented it too quickly. Nike\xe2\x80\x99s market cap is now down to $113 billion as it rebuilds, and Donahue is gone. (The company\xe2\x80\x99s annual revenue for the fiscal year ending in May 2024 was $51 billion\xe2\x80\x94interestingly, a $6 billion-plus improvement from the pandemic peak years.) The good news, of course, is that Nike is still way larger than everyone else, as BMO analyst Simeon Siegel recently reminded me, and is already making the appropriate pivots under new C.E.O. Elliott Hill , a former company lifer who was brought out of retirement about two months after Lauren\xe2\x80\x99s piece was published.

The Jordan Rules

Based on my many conversations, Nike\xe2\x80\x99s path forward is pretty simple. First, the company needs a new dominant performance shoe, something on par with Bowerman\xe2\x80\x99s initial innovation. (Nike hasn\xe2\x80\x99t had a true breakthrough since the Flyknit sneaker, which debuted in 2012. In the same period, Adidas introduced its \xe2\x80\x9cBoost\xe2\x80\x9d technology and the Yeezy.) Second, they need to balance their legacy products, particularly Jordan sneakers, by creating scarcity without sacrificing scale. (I\xe2\x80\x99m constantly amazed that my 9-year-old son is obsessed with a shoe that debuted more than three decades before he was born.) The current \xe2\x80\x9c Unbannable \xe2\x80\x9d campaign suggests that Nike is once again leaning into the lore of the Jordan brand.


Consequentially, Nike also needs to push more deeply into the lifestyle category that it appears to have ignored for years, and which is now the domain of, among others, Lululemon, Alo, and even Vuori. These brands recognized that performance gear is an annual purchase, but consumers refresh their lifestyle wear much more often.

Perhaps most importantly, Hill needs to reestablish Nike\xe2\x80\x99s deep relationships with retail partners and an understanding that great global brands require multiple channels to thrive. Hill, who previously led Nike\xe2\x80\x99s consumer, sales, and North American businesses, knows that the next stage of growth can\xe2\x80\x99t come from D.T.C. alone. While Nike\xe2\x80\x99s D.T.C. push made sense on paper five years ago, retail is ultimately a business where you need to meet customers where they are shopping.

Nike may have been forged as a performance brand, but it\xe2\x80\x99s a company built on design and distribution. Sure, they have their own stores, but they ultimately act as massive marketing billboards and experiential retail. Their Well Collective concept stores, for instance, transform traditional retail spaces into wellness hubs where customers can explore fitness journeys through interactive digital experiences\xe2\x80\x94and, of course, still pick up their online orders in-store. Hill gets this.

He\xe2\x80\x99s already rebuilding bridges with key wholesale partners, like Dick\xe2\x80\x99s and Foot Locker. During a recent channel check, in fact, Nike still had more than 50 percent of the selling floor in Foot Locker, including an entire annex room, \xe2\x80\x9cHouse of Hoops,\xe2\x80\x9d that felt like a shrine to Jordans. After being notably absent for a spell from The Running Event, an industry-wide trade show in San Antonio, Nike strategically returned this year with a shoe that seems to have learned a thing or two from its competitors. The Zoom Fly 6 features Hoka-like cushioning, a move straight from the company\xe2\x80\x99s historical playbook of observing, improving, and scaling successful innovations.

After all, in Nike\xe2\x80\x99s history, the company doesn\xe2\x80\x99t always move first, but it almost always finds a way to offer a superior product. A perfect example, one former Nike insider pointed out, is the Nike Pro line, a $2 billion business that started as a response to Under Armour\xe2\x80\x99s successful introduction of \xe2\x80\x9cspandex,\xe2\x80\x9d a.k.a. compression, into the men\xe2\x80\x99s performance category. Under Armour may have pioneered compression wear, but Nike\xe2\x80\x99s ability to scale and normalize the category turned an innovation into everyday athletic wear.

What We\xe2\x80\x99re Reading\xe2\x80\xa6 and Listening To\xe2\x80\xa6

Once again, Hillary Kerr \xe2\x80\x99s interview with ShopMy co-founder and C.O.O. Tiffany Lopinsky showcased her gift for getting people to give actionable business advice. (Not easy in this over-media-trained world.) Lopinksy offered an incisive explanation of the changing dynamics between creators and brands, and how ShopMy is making it easier for both to make more money. [ Second Life ]

Writer Halie LeSavage is a Copenhagen Fashion Week expert at this point, and this season, she unearthed dead-stock Chanel tweed from Caroline Bille Brahe and gave the city high marks for runway model diversity. [ Marie Claire ]

The Met Gala\xe2\x80\x99s dress code is essentially\xe2\x80\xa6 wear a suit . [ AP ]

Apparently, a lot of women find the presence of men at Solidcore annoying. (Just don\xe2\x80\x99t sit next to them?) Also, it\xe2\x80\x99s dark in there, and it\xe2\x80\x99s not a sweaty class, so it doesn\xe2\x80\x99t smell the way SoulCycle does when there are men in the room. [ The Cut ]

On the GLP-1 beat: Dan Frommer , Lauren \xe2\x80\x99s husband, analyzed some stats around Ozempic usage and spending. Essentially, spending on food and related things goes down the first year, but then creeps back up. Perhaps a lot of people stop taking the drugs after a year? The point: GLP-1s might curb cravings overall, but the effects are short-term, for now. [ The New Consumer ]

That\xe2\x80\x99s it from Sarah and me. R.I.P. to Biologique Recherche P50 1970, which was finally banned in the United States thanks to new F.D.A. regulations that don\xe2\x80\x99t allow the exfoliating agent phenol in skincare. (The other versions are still good.) Congrats to all the romantic partners of the people who used P50 1970 because they will finally rid themselves of that notorious smell. (I likened it to the burn-off from a barbecue. My husband says embalming fluid.) Get yours here while you still can.

Until tomorrow,

Lauren


P.S. : We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off them.

An insider-friendly tip sheet from Matthew Belloni, who spent 14 years in the trenches at The Hollywood Reporter and five before that as an entertainment lawyer. Subscribers also receive What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing+, a companion email focused on entertainment law, the streaming industry, and more.
